channel.title: Development Data Partnership
channel.description: Recent content on Development Data Partnership

item.title: The Digitalization Transition in Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Mexico and Peru Through the Lens of the LinkedIn Skill Genome Data
item.description: A World Bank study funded by the Digital Development Trust Fund examined how advanced digital skills emerged, evolved, and were adopted in six Latin American countries by using LinkedIn data.

item.title: Uncovering the Digital Divide in Brazil: Data Reveals Internet Inequality Across Income Groups
item.description: Leveraging data from Ookla for Good™, the World Bank’s Digital Development Global Practice has analyzed the relationship between internet speed and income inequality within a sample of Brazilian cities.

item.title: For Reduced Exposure to COVID-19, the Quality of Your Internet Matters
item.description: During the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, governments-imposed movement restrictions to curb the spread of the disease. With access to the internet, people could work or study remotely, therefore making it easier for them to adhere to pandemic-related movement restrictions.
